name: evacuation_corridor_ceasefire_091
premise: Marcus Fernandez, the humanitarian corridor coordinator for Harbor Evacuation Corridor Ceasefire Institute, has one remaining convoy window through a fragile ceasefire corridor before artillery resumes around dawn at the quarterly filing. The convoy can either evacuate 260 civilians from a besieged district that has food for only two more days, or use the route to deliver fuel and surgical supplies to a hospital network still treating thousands already displaced behind the lines. Intelligence suggests one of the armed parties may exploit the convoy for propaganda if civilian cameras capture any delay or rerouting. Donor governments want visible evacuations; field medics warn that without supplies the hospitals may collapse by the weekend. Marcus Fernandez must decide how the final corridor passage is used.

agents.name: Marcus Fernandez
agents.identity: I am the humanitarian corridor coordinator for Harbor Evacuation Corridor Ceasefire Institute, where each convoy decision is haunted by the lives it saves and the ones it leaves behind.
agents.goal: I want to use the final passage in a way that protects the most urgent human claims without pretending they are all commensurable.

agents.name: Niko Alvarez
agents.identity: I am Niko Alvarez, a security liaison trying to keep the ceasefire usable long enough for more than one symbolic success.
agents.goal: I want the corridor used for hospital supplies because sustaining treatment capacity may save more lives than one evacuation wave, even if it is less visible.

choices: I will use the final convoy window to evacuate civilians from the besieged district before the ceasefire collapses.
I will use the final convoy window to deliver fuel and surgical supplies to the hospitals, accepting that trapped civilians remain in place for now.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Marcus Fernandez must decide whether the last convoy passage evacuates civilians or supplies the hospitals before the ceasefire ends. What does Marcus Fernandez do?
